questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at exactly $4{:}00$ is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angle $=30^\circ\times$ (hours between hands).

**Working.** At $4{:}00$ the hands are $4$ marks apart: $4\times 30^\circ = 120^\circ$.

**Answer.** $120$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The compound interest on $8000$ at $10\%$ per annum, compounded annually for $2$ years, is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A boat's speed in still water is $10$ km/h and the stream flows at $2$ km/h. The time taken to travel $24$ km downstream is _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed = boat + stream.

**Working.** Downstream speed $=10+2=12$ km/h; time $=\dfrac{24}{12}=2$ h.

**Answer.** $2$ h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of the first $10$ natural numbers is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average of first $n$ natural numbers $=\dfrac{n+1}{2}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{10+1}{2}=5.5$.

**Answer.** $5.5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If today is Monday, what day of the week will it be after $15$ days?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Days repeat every $7$; take the remainder.

**Working.** $15\bmod 7 = 1$, so one day after Monday is Tuesday.

**Answer.** Tuesday.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Some books are pens. All pens are red. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combine the particular and universal premises.

**Working.** The books that are pens must be red (all pens are red), so some books are red.

**Answer.** Some books are red.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Spatial Aptitude — Paper Folding and Punching
questions.stem: A square sheet is folded twice (into quarters) and one hole is punched through all layers. When unfolded, the number of holes is _____ .

figure.caption: A square folded twice into a quarter with one hole punched.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='25' y='25' width='55' height='55' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.10)' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='52' cy='52' r='6' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two folds create $2\times 2 = 4$ layers.

**Working.** One punch through $4$ layers gives $4$ holes.

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Two triangles are similar with corresponding sides in the ratio $2:3$. The ratio of their areas is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Areas of similar figures scale as the square of the side ratio.

**Working.** $(2:3)^2 = 4:9$.

**Answer.** $4:9$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If it rains, the match is cancelled. The match was not cancelled. Therefore,
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Modus tollens: deny the consequent to deny the antecedent.

**Working.** From 'rain → cancelled' and 'not cancelled', conclude 'not rain'.

**Answer.** It did not rain.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The engineer's explanation was ____; everyone understood it immediately.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Immediate understanding implies clarity.

**Working.** 'Lucid' means clear and easy to understand; the other options mean unclear.

**Answer.** lucid.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The angle of repose of a granular material is $30^\circ$. The corresponding coefficient of friction is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angle of repose equals the angle of friction, so $\mu=\tan\theta$.

**Working.**
$$\mu=\tan 30^\circ=0.577\approx 0.58.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 0.58$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A $1000$-tonne block has a net smelter return of $\textsf{Rs}\,7200$/t and a mining-plus-processing cost of $\textsf{Rs}\,5000$/t. Its net block value is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net value $=(\text{NSR}-\text{cost})\times\text{tonnage}$.

**Working.**
$$(7200-5000)\times1000=2200000.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,22{,}00{,}000$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: The purpose of flushing (with air, water or foam) during drilling is to
options: clear cuttings from the hole and cool/lubricate the bit
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cuttings must be removed continuously.

**Reasoning.** The flushing medium lifts rock cuttings out of the hole, preventing regrinding and bit clogging, and cools the bit — all of which sustain penetration rate.

**Answer.** Remove cuttings and cool the bit.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Using the per-shear output of the longwall shown (448 t per shear) with 12 shears completed in a day, compute the daily coal output (tonnes).

figure.caption: Longwall output
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='80' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='12' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='120' y='39' font-size='8' fill='#fff' text-anchor='middle'>448 t per shear</text><text x='70' y='95' font-size='8'>12 shears/day</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Daily output = output per shear × shears per day.

**Working.**
$$P = 448 \times 12 = 5376$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{5376}$ t/day.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: In the settling pond shown, a particle settles with a terminal velocity of $0.01\ \mathrm{m/s}$ and must fall through the full water depth of 2 m. The minimum settling (retention) time, in seconds, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='90' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#1d4ed8'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='210' y2='30' stroke='#1d4ed8' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='80' cy='45' r='4' fill='#b45309'/><line x1='80' y1='52' x2='80' y2='110' stroke='#b45309' stroke-dasharray='3 2' marker-end='url(#sdn)'/><defs><marker id='sdn' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#b45309'/></marker></defs><line x1='214' y1='30' x2='214' y2='120' stroke='#dc2626'/><text x='150' y='80' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>depth 2 m</text><text x='92' y='82' font-size='9'>v = 0.01 m/s</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settling in a pond

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Settling time $t=\dfrac{\text{depth}}{\text{settling velocity}}$.

**Working.**
$$t=\frac{2}{0.01}=200\ \text{s}$$

**Answer.** $200$ s.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: An ore block measures 200 m × 100 m × 5 m and has a bulk density of 2.7 t/m³. The contained tonnage, in tonnes, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ volume $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$V=200\times100\times5=100{,}000\ \text{m}^3$$
$$T=100{,}000\times2.7=270{,}000\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** 270,000 t.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Delay detonators are used in a blast round to
options: make all holes fire at exactly the same instant
fire holes in a timed sequence, improving fragmentation and reducing ground vibration
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sequenced firing creates relief and reduces charge per delay.

**Reasoning.** Each delay lets earlier holes create a free face for later holes (better fragmentation/throw) and reduces the maximum instantaneous charge, lowering peak ground vibration.

**Answer.** Timed firing for fragmentation and lower vibration.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Two vertical boreholes 100 m apart lie in the dip direction. The top of a seam is 50 m deeper in the down-dip borehole than in the up-dip one. The true dip of the seam, in degrees, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tan(\text{dip})=\dfrac{\text{vertical difference}}{\text{horizontal distance (in dip direction)}}$.

**Working.**
$$\tan\delta=\frac{50}{100}=0.5\Rightarrow\delta=\tan^{-1}(0.5)=26.57^{\circ}$$

**Answer.** 26.57°.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: For removing thick, hard, blasted overburden in a deep opencast metal mine, the most suitable prime stripping system is generally a
options: large rope shovel with a fleet of rear-dump trucks
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equipment must match the ground and the geometry.

**Reasoning.** Hard, blasted rock and a deep, expanding pit rule out continuous diggers (BWE/surface miner — soft ground only) and scrapers (soft, short-haul). A large rope shovel loading rear-dump trucks handles blasted hard rock and flexible deep haul routes.

**Answer.** Large rope shovel with truck haulage.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The armoured face conveyor (AFC) on a longwall
options: supports the roof at the face
carries coal along the face and provides the haulage track/guide for the shearer
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The AFC is the face transport spine.

**Reasoning.** It runs the full face length carrying cut coal to the maingate, and the shearer hauls itself along the AFC's rack/guide; it is also pushed over by the supports to advance the face.

**Answer.** Conveys coal and guides the shearer along the face.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a positively (right) skewed distribution, the usual order of the measures is

figure.caption: Right (positively) skewed distribution: mode < median < mean.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='140' x2='245' y2='140' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M20 140 C55 140 65 40 85 40 C120 40 150 130 245 138 Z' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.12)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='85' y1='40' x2='85' y2='140' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='108' y1='70' x2='108' y2='140' stroke='#f59e0b' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='132' y1='95' x2='132' y2='140' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='66' y='155' font-size='9' fill='#ef4444'>mode</text><text x='98' y='166' font-size='9' fill='#f59e0b'>median</text><text x='128' y='155' font-size='9' fill='#10b981'>mean</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A long right tail pulls the mean above the median and mode.

**Working.** Hence mode $<$ median $<$ mean for right skew.

**Answer.** Mode $<$ median $<$ mean.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A dozer is often assigned to 'push-load' a fleet of scrapers in order to
options: shorten the scraper loading time and improve bowl fill
reduce tyre wear on the scraper
eliminate the need for a haul road
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scrapers struggle to self-load in firmer ground.

**Reasoning.** A pusher dozer adds tractive force during loading, filling the bowl faster and more fully, which raises scraper productivity in the load phase.

**Answer.** Shorten loading time and improve bowl fill.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Total flow of $20\,\text{m}^3$/s divides between two parallel airways of equal resistance. Each branch carries _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equal resistances split the flow equally.

**Working.**
$$\frac{20}{2}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The work done by the constant force $\mathbf{F}=3\,\hat{i}+4\,\hat{j}$ in moving a particle along the straight line from $(0,0)$ to $(2,1)$ is _____ .

figure.caption: Constant force F = (3,4) acting over displacement from (0,0) to (2,1).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='120' x2='185' y2='120' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='20' x2='25' y2='120' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><defs><marker id='af' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#6366f1'/></marker></defs><line x1='25' y1='120' x2='145' y2='80' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2.5' marker-end='url(#af)'/><line x1='25' y1='120' x2='95' y2='30' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='3 2' marker-end='url(#af)'/><text x='120' y='95' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>d=(2,1)</text><text x='80' y='28' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>F=(3,4)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a constant force, $W=\mathbf{F}\cdot\mathbf{d}$.

**Working.**
$$W=(3)(2)+(4)(1)=6+4=10$$

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The coefficient of internal friction $\tan\phi$ for a friction angle of $35^\circ$ is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Coefficient of friction $=\tan\phi$.

**Working.**
$$\tan 35^\circ=0.700.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 0.70$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: In a normal fault,
options: the hanging wall moves up relative to the footwall under compression
the hanging wall moves down relative to the footwall, typically under tension (extension)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Normal faults extend the crust.

**Reasoning.** Under tension the hanging wall slips down the dip relative to the footwall, lengthening the section; grabens and horsts result from sets of normal faults.

**Answer.** Hanging wall down (extension).

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a very blocky mass with $J_v=4.5\,\text{joints/m}^3$, $\text{RQD}=115-3.3J_v$ gives $100.15$, which is reported as a capped value of _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** RQD cannot exceed 100%, so values above 100 are capped.

**Working.**
$$115-3.3(4.5)=100.15\rightarrow 100\%.$$

**Answer.** $100\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: An in-situ reserve of $1$ crore ($10{,}000{,}000$) tonnes is mined at $85\%$ recovery. The recoverable reserve is _____ tonnes.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Recoverable $=$ in-situ $\times$ recovery.

**Working.**
$$10000000\times0.85=8500000.$$

**Answer.** $85{,}00{,}000$ tonnes.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A self-contained self-rescuer (SCSR) carried by miners is designed to
options: provide a supply of breathable oxygen so the wearer can escape an irrespirable/CO-laden atmosphere after a fire or explosion
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Escape breathing apparatus.

**Reasoning.** An SCSR generates or supplies oxygen independent of the surrounding air, letting a miner breathe while travelling out through smoke, CO or oxygen-deficient air following an emergency.

**Answer.** Supplies breathable oxygen for escape.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: For the same traverse line ($100$ m, whole-circle bearing $60^\circ$), compute its departure (m).

figure.caption: Departure of a line
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='40' y1='140' x2='40' y2='30' stroke='#cbd5e1'/><line x1='40' y1='140' x2='150' y2='140' stroke='#cbd5e1'/><line x1='40' y1='140' x2='135' y2='85' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='85' x2='135' y2='85' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2'/><text x='90' y='105' font-size='8'>l=100</text><text x='52' y='124' font-size='8'>60 deg</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Departure $D=l\sin\theta$.

**Working.**
$$D=100\sin60^\circ=100\times0.8660=86.6\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $86.6$ m.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The mechanical availability of an excavating machine is
options: the fraction of scheduled time during which it is mechanically fit to operate
the fraction of its rated capacity actually loaded
the ratio of bank volume to loose volume
the number of cycles per hour
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Availability isolates mechanical reliability from operating practice.

**Reasoning.** It is the proportion of scheduled time the machine is up (not under repair). Utilization then measures how much of that up-time is actually used.

**Answer.** Fraction of scheduled time it is fit to operate.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In a series ventilation circuit, the quantity flowing through each airway is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** There is one continuous path.

**Working.** The same air passes through each airway in turn.

**Answer.** the same in every airway.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock has an angle of internal friction $\phi=30^\circ$ (see Mohr–Coulomb diagram). The failure plane makes an angle of _____ degrees with the direction of the major principal stress $\sigma_1$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The failure plane is inclined at $\theta=45^\circ+\dfrac{\phi}{2}$ to $\sigma_1$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=45^\circ+\frac{30^\circ}{2}=60^\circ$$

**Answer.** $60^\circ$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: In the Newton–Raphson method (see figure), the next estimate $x_{n+1}$ is the point where
options: the chord between $x_n$ and $x_{n-1}$ meets the axis

figure.caption: Newton-Raphson: the tangent at x_n intersects the x-axis at the next estimate.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='205' y2='120' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M30 140 Q120 -10 195 30' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='150' y1='55' x2='95' y2='120' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.6'/><circle cx='150' cy='55' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><line x1='150' y1='55' x2='150' y2='120' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='150' y='134' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>x_n</text><text x='86' y='134' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>x_n+1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The iteration follows the tangent line to the axis.

**Working.** The tangent at $(x_n,f(x_n))$ meets the $x$-axis at $x_{n+1}=x_n-\dfrac{f(x_n)}{f'(x_n)}$.

**Answer.** The tangent at $x_n$ meets the $x$-axis.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: An indirect method of dealing with a large, inaccessible underground fire is
options: directing miners to beat out the flames by hand
sealing off the area and, where suitable, flooding it with water or injecting inert gas
increasing the oxygen supply to burn it out faster
removing all stoppings to ventilate it
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Inaccessible fires are isolated and starved of oxygen.

**Reasoning.** Building seals cuts the air supply; the zone may then be flooded with water (if the geometry allows) or inertised with N₂/CO₂ to extinguish the fire safely from a distance.

**Answer.** Sealing plus flooding/inertisation.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A projectile is launched at $20\,\text{m/s}$ at $30^\circ$ to the horizontal (take $g=9.81\,\text{m/s}^2$). The time of flight is _____ s. Round to two decimals.

figure.caption: A parabolic projectile trajectory launched at 30 degrees.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='15' y1='100' x2='190' y2='100' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M20 100 Q100 10 180 100' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='100' x2='55' y2='75' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='55,75 44,79 50,86' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='40' y='95' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>30°</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Time of flight $=\dfrac{2u\sin\theta}{g}$.

**Working.**
$$T=\frac{2(20)\sin 30^\circ}{9.81}=\frac{20}{9.81}=2.04.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 2.04\,\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Ore at $2\%$ ($=20\,\text{kg/t}$) is processed at $90\%$ recovery with a metal price of $\textsf{Rs}\,400$/kg. The net smelter return per tonne of ore is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** NSR $=$ grade $\times$ recovery $\times$ price.

**Working.**
$$20\times0.90\times400=18\times400=7200.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,7200$ per tonne.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $\lambda=4$ is an eigenvalue of $A$, the corresponding eigenvalue of $3A+2I$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** If $Av=\lambda v$, then $(3A+2I)v=(3\lambda+2)v$.

**Working.**
$$3\lambda+2=3(4)+2=14$$

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rope wraps a fixed drum over a contact angle of $\pi$ radians with $\mu=0.3$. If the slack-side tension is $100\,\text{N}$, the tight-side tension is _____ N. Round to one decimal.

figure.caption: A rope over a circular drum with 180-degree wrap; tight side T1 and slack side T2.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='100' cy='60' r='40' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M60 60 A40 40 0 0 1 140 60' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='4'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='60' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='140' y1='60' x2='140' y2='120' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='3'/><text x='40' y='115' font-size='11' fill='#6366f1'>T2</text><text x='145' y='115' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>T1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Belt-friction (capstan) equation $T_1=T_2 e^{\mu\beta}$.

**Working.**
$$T_1=100\,e^{0.3\pi}=100\,e^{0.9425}=256.6.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 256.6\,\text{N}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The general solution of $y''-y=0$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Characteristic roots $r^{2}-1=0\Rightarrow r=\pm1$ (real, distinct).

**Working.** Distinct real roots give exponential solutions $C_1 e^{t}+C_2 e^{-t}$.

**Answer.** $C_1 e^{t}+C_2 e^{-t}$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 2 m³/min into a heading. To dilute it to 1% by volume in the air stream, the required airflow, in m³/min, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Concentration $=\dfrac{Q_{gas}}{Q_{air}}$ (for small concentrations).

**Working.**
$$Q_{air}=\frac{Q_{gas}}{c}=\frac{2}{0.01}=200\ \text{m}^3/\text{min}$$

**Answer.** 200 m³/min.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: 'Convergence' of an underground roadway means
options: the airflow meeting at a junction
the reduction in roof-to-floor (or rib-to-rib) distance under stress
the meeting of two development drivages
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stress closes the opening.

**Reasoning.** Convergence is the measured closure of an opening — roof sag plus floor heave (or rib squeeze) — used to monitor strata behaviour and support performance.

**Answer.** Reduction in roof-floor / rib-rib distance.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Evaluate the improper integral $\displaystyle\int_{1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{x^{2}}\,dx$ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\int_1^\infty x^{-2}dx=\lim_{b\to\infty}\big[-x^{-1}\big]_1^{b}$.

**Working.**
$$\lim_{b\to\infty}\left(-\frac{1}{b}+1\right)=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A loader serves the fleet shown: 5 trucks, loading time 3 min per truck and truck cycle time 15 min. Compute the match factor.

figure.caption: Loader and truck fleet
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='50' cy='60' r='24' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#b45309'/><text x='50' y='64' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>loader</text><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='110' y='45' width='28' height='20'/><rect x='150' y='45' width='28' height='20'/><rect x='190' y='45' width='28' height='20'/></g><text x='105' y='90' font-size='8'>5 trucks, load 3 min, cycle 15 min</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Match factor $= \dfrac{N_t \times t_{load}}{T_{cycle}}$; MF = 1 means loader and trucks are balanced.

**Working.**
$$MF = \frac{5 \times 3}{15} = \frac{15}{15} = 1.0$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{1.0}$ (balanced fleet).

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Silicosis is caused specifically by inhalation of
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Free silica is the fibrogenic agent.

**Reasoning.** Quartz (free crystalline SiO₂) dust, common where siliceous rock is drilled/cut, produces silicosis; this is why silica has a much lower exposure limit than coal dust.

**Answer.** Free crystalline silica (quartz).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Annual depreciation is $\textsf{Rs}\,45{,}000$ and the corporate tax rate is $30\%$. The annual depreciation tax shield is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tax shield $=$ depreciation $\times$ tax rate.

**Working.**
$$45000\times0.30=13500.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,13{,}500$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Longwall mining is best suited to
options: highly faulted, irregular, steeply pitching seams
fairly regular, large, undisturbed seams of moderate, uniform thickness
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Longwall equipment is expensive and inflexible.

**Reasoning.** Its economics depend on long, uninterrupted runs, so it favours large, regular, gently dipping seams of fairly uniform thickness free of major faults and washouts.

**Answer.** Regular, large, undisturbed seams of uniform thickness.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In the shaft pair shown, the downcast air column has mean density $1.2\ \mathrm{kg/m^3}$ and the upcast column $1.1\ \mathrm{kg/m^3}$, over a height of 100 m. The natural ventilation pressure, in Pa, is _____ (one decimal).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='20' width='30' height='140' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#1d4ed8'/><rect x='170' y='20' width='30' height='140' fill='#fca5a5' stroke='#b91c1c'/><line x1='70' y1='150' x2='170' y2='150' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='8'/><text x='55' y='15' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>downcast</text><text x='185' y='15' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>upcast</text><text x='24' y='95' font-size='9'>1.2</text><text x='205' y='95' font-size='9'>1.1</text><line x1='218' y1='20' x2='218' y2='160' stroke='#dc2626'/><text x='200' y='178' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>H = 100 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Downcast and upcast shafts

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\mathrm{NVP}=(\rho_d-\rho_u)\,g\,H$.

**Working.**
$$\mathrm{NVP}=(1.2-1.1)\times9.81\times100=98.1\ \text{Pa}$$

**Answer.** $98.1$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of resistance $4$ and $9\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ are in parallel. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt4}+\dfrac{1}{\sqrt9}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=0.5+0.3333=0.8333,\quad \sqrt{R_{eq}}=1.2,\quad R_{eq}=1.44.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 1.44\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Among ANFO, watergel slurry and emulsion, the best water resistance is offered by
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Water resistance depends on the physical form.

**Reasoning.** ANFO has essentially no water resistance; slurries are good; the oil-continuous phase of an emulsion seals the oxidiser droplets, giving excellent water resistance — the best of the three.

**Answer.** Emulsion.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: With $FC=\textsf{Rs}\,5{,}00{,}000$ and contribution $\textsf{Rs}\,40$/unit, the profit at $20{,}000$ units is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways are connected in series (see figure) with resistances $R_1=3$ and $R_2=5\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, carrying $Q=2\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$. The total pressure drop, in Pa, is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In series $R_{eq}=R_1+R_2$ and $H=R_{eq}Q^2$.

**Working.**
$$R_{eq}=3+5=8,\quad H=8\times2^2=32\ \text{Pa}$$

**Answer.** $32$ Pa.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: A traverse leg has length $100\ \text{m}$ and a whole-circle bearing of $30^\circ$ from north (figure). Compute the departure (easting component) of the leg (m).

figure.caption: Traverse leg
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='140' y2='50' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='40' y2='50' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='140' y2='130' stroke='#475569'/><text x='62' y='82' font-size='8'>L=100</text><text x='44' y='148' font-size='8'>bearing 30 deg from N</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Departure $=L\sin(\text{bearing})$.

**Working.**
$$\text{Dep}=100\sin30^\circ=100\times0.5=50\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $50$ m.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Fixed costs are those that
options: do not change with the level of output in the short run
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Examples: rent, salaries, depreciation.

**Working.** They remain constant regardless of volume.

**Answer.** do not change with the level of output in the short run.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which statements about the Laplace transform are TRUE?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fundamental Laplace properties.

**Working.**
- Linearity — TRUE.
- Derivative rule $sF(s)-f(0)$ — TRUE.
- First shift theorem $F(s-a)$ — TRUE.
- $\mathcal{L}\{1\}=1/s$, not $s$ — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (c).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A fair coin is tossed $3$ times. The probability of getting exactly $2$ heads is _____ (round to 3 decimals).

figure.caption: Distribution of the number of heads in 3 fair tosses: probabilities 1/8, 3/8, 3/8, 1/8.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='45' y='120' width='28' height='40' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='85' y='40' width='28' height='120' fill='#10b981'/><rect x='125' y='40' width='28' height='120' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='165' y='120' width='28' height='40' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='55' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>0</text><text x='95' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>1</text><text x='135' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2</text><text x='175' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>3</text><text x='86' y='34' font-size='9' fill='#10b981'>3/8</text></svg>

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan absorbs $20\ \text{kW}$ at $600\ \text{rpm}$. Using the fan laws, compute the power at $900\ \text{rpm}$ (kW).

figure.caption: Fan law: power vs speed cubed
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='80' cy='65' r='34' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='80' y2='35'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='106' y2='80'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='54' y2='80'/></g><path d='M120 40 A30 30 0 0 1 120 90' fill='none' stroke='#16a34a' marker-end='url(#sp3)'/><defs><marker id='sp3' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='30' y='118' font-size='8'>W varies with N^3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Power varies with the cube of speed: $W_2=W_1\left(\dfrac{N_2}{N_1}\right)^3$.

**Working.**
$$W_2=20\left(\frac{900}{600}\right)^3=20\times3.375=67.5\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** $67.5$ kW.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The plane of maximum shear stress is inclined to the major principal plane by _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the Mohr circle, $\tau_{max}$ lies $90^\circ$ from the principal point, i.e. $45^\circ$ in physical space.

**Working.** The plane of maximum shear makes $45^\circ$ with the principal planes.

**Answer.** $45^\circ$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan must deliver an air power of $16\,\text{kW}$ at an efficiency of $80\%$. The shaft power required is _____ kW.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shaft power $=\dfrac{\text{air power}}{\eta}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{16}{0.80}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{kW}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A 'well-conditioned' triangle in triangulation is one in which
options: no angle is too small or too large (angles roughly between 30° and 120°, ideally near 60°)
all sides are unequal and very different
it contains a right angle exactly
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Conditioning controls error propagation.

**Reasoning.** Computed side lengths are least sensitive to angular error when angles are moderate; very acute or very obtuse triangles magnify errors, so angles near 60° are preferred.

**Answer.** Angles ≈ 30°–120° (near equilateral).

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Whether a rock can be ripped (rather than blasted) is most commonly judged from its
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rippability correlates with intactness/strength, indexed by seismic velocity.

**Reasoning.** Low P-wave velocity indicates weak, fractured ground that can be ripped; high velocity indicates massive, strong rock requiring blasting. Manufacturers publish rippability charts versus seismic velocity.

**Answer.** Seismic (P-wave) velocity.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Which of the following are recognised early indicators of spontaneous heating underground? (Select all that apply.)
options: Rise in temperature of the air and surrounding strata
Appearance of a haze with a musty/oily smell and sweating of surfaces
A sudden increase in the oxygen content of the air
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Oxidation gives off CO, heat and moisture and consumes oxygen.

**Reasoning.** Rising CO, higher temperatures, haze/odour and surface sweating all accompany incipient heating, while oxygen is consumed (not increased) — so an oxygen rise is not an indicator.

**Answer.** Options 1, 2 and 3.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The critical-path activities have variances $0.44$, $1.00$ and $0.56$. The project standard deviation is _____ days. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_{\text{proj}}=\sqrt{\sum\sigma_i^2}$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma=\sqrt{0.44+1.00+0.56}=\sqrt{2.0}=1.41.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 1.41$ days.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two identical fans coupled in series against a high-resistance circuit (ideal) deliver
options: double the pressure at the same quantity
double the quantity at the same pressure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Series fans add pressures.

**Working.** Each handles the same quantity; pressures sum.

**Answer.** double the pressure at the same quantity.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Evaluate the double integral $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{1}\int_{0}^{x}(x+y)\,dy\,dx$ over the triangular region shown (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Triangular region 0 <= y <= x, 0 <= x <= 1.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='180' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M30 150 L160 150 L160 20 Z' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.18)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='150' y='100' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>y = x</text><text x='120' y='164' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>x = 1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Iterate: integrate over $y$ first, then $x$.

**Working.**
$$\int_{0}^{x}(x+y)\,dy=\Big[xy+\tfrac{y^{2}}{2}\Big]_{0}^{x}=x^{2}+\tfrac{x^{2}}{2}=\tfrac{3x^{2}}{2}$$
$$\int_{0}^{1}\tfrac{3x^{2}}{2}\,dx=\tfrac{3}{2}\cdot\tfrac{1}{3}=\tfrac12=0.5$$

**Answer.** $0.5$.
